items.title: Alleged hit-and-run driver gave himself up after freeway death, police say

children.content: A 22-year-old man dies after being struck by a car on the Mitchell Freeway in Perth's north, with the alleged driver charged after he later went to a police station, as part of the freeway remains shut.

gallery.altText: A wide shot of an empty strethc of the Mitchell Freeway after a fatla crash, with several vehicles on either side of the road.
A yellow 'Road closed' sign sits on the road in front of traffic at an intersection.

thumbnail.altText: A wide shot of an empty strethc of the Mitchell Freeway after a fatla crash, with several vehicles on either side of the road.
A yellow 'Road closed' sign sits on the road in front of traffic at an intersection.

items.title: Perth's Cottesloe revamp to go ahead but warning more funding needed

children.content: An oceanfront redevelopment at Perth's iconic Cottesloe Beach will go ahead with $15 million in funding, but the mayor says more financial assistance is needed from the state government to complete the project.

image.altText: Two women with one wearing hijab siting up and a couple next to them lying on the lawn by beach.

items.title: Exhibition sheds fresh light on WA's pre-colonial history

children.content: Oral histories from Menang Noongar elders are combined with AI renderings of life before colonisation in an exhibition at the WA State Library.

image.altText: Woman with bright orange jacket stands in front of bushland

items.title: Black mould and broken toilets: Inside 'dismal' government-owned homes

children.content: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

gallery.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.
A man walking towards a tin building with palm trees along the side.
A kitchen sink with broken cupboards underneath and a window above covered by dirty sheet

items.title: How a container of hot chocolate put the spotlight on airport security

children.content: A security scare that shut down a Victorian airport last week was found to be a false alarm.

image.altText: Passengers line up for security screening at the airport.

items.title: The migration maths behind the housing crisis is not what politicians claim

children.content: Arthur Calwell argued migration was essential to national survival. Today, the Coalition says it should depend on how many homes get built, but the economics are far from straightforward.

image.altText: a male politician giving a speech in a chamber

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: 'It's atrocious, the apathy': Survivor calls on governments to act

children.content: At nine years old, Bronwyn was sent to a notorious government-run children's home without her parents' consent. What unfolded next impacted the rest of her life.

image.altText: A profile photo of a woman looking directly at the camera.

items.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after IPL on-field incident

children.content: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

image.altText: Australian batsman Travis head looks at his bat while Indian fielder Virat Kohli smiles and shouts.

items.title: The scale of the mouse plague is hard to fathom. Here's a visualisation

children.content: A mouse plague is invading farms and homes in southern and Western Australia. But what does that really look like?

gallery.altText: A graphic showing a top-down view of a bedroom where several mice have invaded.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic showing a top-down view of a bedroom where several mice have invaded.

items.title: McLaren 'looked like idiots', Brit feels fate is ruining his F1 title dream

children.content: McLaren has "looked like idiots" after a bold tyre choice at the start of the Canadian Grand Prix backfired immediately for its two drivers.

image.altText: F1 driver in the garage, wearing a jacket, exhausted after a race

items.title: Data shows why Australians are stuck and not switching jobs or starting businesses

children.content: People are less mobile, less dynamic and more risk-averse than we used to be, a series of economic indicators shows.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses, seated in front of a laptop

items.title: Link between terrorism and DV could be key to preventing homicide

children.content: A world-first research project is aiming to prevent family and intimate partner homicides with similar strategies to those being used to tackle terrorism and lone-actor attacks.

items.title: WA health official wants more support to stem 'rare' diphtheria outbreak

children.content: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

image.altText: A nurse preparing a boostrix vaccination, the vaccine's white and purple box is visible in the foreground.

items.title: Death of 'drongo': Are Aussie insults and swearwords dying out?

children.content: Classic Australian swearwords and insults are becoming less popular with younger generations. What does losing them mean for our culture?

items.title: A movie about mystery-hunting sheep delights this May

children.content: Plus the rom-com of yore returns in Finding Emily, the farmyard mystery that's been delighting audiences, and a gory Hollywood video game adaptation filmed on the Gold Coast.

items.title: What life is like without alcohol

children.content: Here's what you had to say about leaving alcohol behind and how it's impacted your health, friendships and lifestyle.

items.title: Psychiatrist repeatedly harassed nurse on messaging platforms

children.content: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

children.content: The Canberra woman says the justice system is unpredictable for victim-survivors but many aspects are worth it.

items.title: Trump's push for a ceasefire is good for peace but our wallets still feel the pain

children.content: US President Donald Trump was yesterday touting an end to the war in Iran, but declaring mission accomplished in Washington will not spare Australian consumers, and politicians, from the economic fallout of the conflict.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson wearing a red suit jacket, speaking at a media conference.

items.title: ASX 200 rises after Dow closes at record high, oil falls under $US100

children.content: The Australian share market has defied expectations to begin the new week in positive territory, after the Dow Jones posted new records. Meanwhile, the price of Brent crude has dropped under $US100 a barrel. Follow our live business blog.

image.altText: ASX board viewed behind a person's head walking past window

items.title: Australia's opening times are world leading and researchers say it's a strength

children.content: Australia has a "distinctive strength" when it comes to early trading, but more knowledge is needed for businesses to fully capitalise on it.

image.altText: A person runs along the Brisbane River in South Bank with people sitting at a coffee van in the background.

items.title: 'No sign of danger': Friend details horror of fatal shark attack

children.content: The best friend of a Perth father killed by a shark off Rottnest Island says there was "absolutely no warning" before the attack.
